surfaces.name: Governed Agent JSON API
guardrails: BBE remains the diagnostic spine.
Only approved dynamic views can be requested.
Source candidates are visible context, not canonical evidence.
surfaces.nextStep: Keep as the reference contract for governed JSON turns.

surfaces.name: Governed Agent Stream API
guardrails: Streaming events expose public operational status, not hidden reasoning.
Voice capture must share the same evidence and gate rails as typed turns.
Continuous listening remains disabled.
surfaces.nextStep: Use as the governed transport for typed and push-to-talk Agent Lab turns.

surfaces.name: Agent Lab Command Center
guardrails: Dynamic workspaces render from ExperiencePlans.
The command center cannot invent arbitrary UI modules.
Browser speech is one prompted turn with typed fallback; continuous voice remains disabled.
surfaces.nextStep: Continue polishing the Jarvis-style interaction while preserving the governed ExperiencePlan contract.

surfaces.name: Scoped Chat Default
guardrails: Keep the current report and default chat stable.
Do not expose governed runtime rails unless explicitly requested.
The LLM can explain but cannot invent diagnoses or treatments.
surfaces.nextStep: Keep as stable fallback until governed runtime becomes the approved default.

surfaces.name: Explicit Skill-Routed Chat
guardrails: Default scoped chat remains unchanged.
Governed turns must return the full runtime payload.
Session persistence is prototype-local JSON only.
surfaces.nextStep: Use to graduate visible chat surfaces one at a time without destabilizing defaults.

surfaces.name: Report Dialog With Data Governed Mode
guardrails: The report remains stable and scoped chat stays default.
Governed mode must show proof under the answer.
Treatments stay options to consider.
surfaces.nextStep: Use opt-in behavior for stakeholder comparison before changing defaults.

surfaces.name: Standalone Brand Conversation Governed Mode
guardrails: Brand conversation stays brand-scoped.
Governed answers must expose evidence gaps and gates.
No diagnosis or treatment invention.
surfaces.nextStep: Use as the richer typed consult surface before enabling continuous voice.

surfaces.name: Live Consult Governed Browser Fallback
surfaces.route: Live Consult panel governed fallback toggle
guardrails: Browser/demo fallback can use the governed runtime.
Realtime voice is not activated by this path.
TTS and continuous listening remain disabled.
surfaces.nextStep: Use as the proof bridge before unifying server-side Realtime voice.

surfaces.name: Live Consult Realtime Candidate
guardrails: Realtime voice cannot bypass governed evidence, memory, audit, or gates.
Server-side cancellation and interruption behavior must be approved first.
API keys stay server-side.
surfaces.nextStep: Unify with runAgentTurn only after provider, consent, privacy, and interruption gates are approved.

surfaces.name: Agent Lab Full Voice Orchestration
guardrails: Do not enable always-listening behavior.
Full voice must preserve the same ExperiencePlan and proof rails as typed turns.
No autonomous speaking without explicit policy approval.
surfaces.nextStep: Design after Realtime adapter parity and interruption behavior are validated.

surfaces.name: Text-To-Speech Provider
guardrails: TTS cannot speak unreviewed artifacts as final recommendations.
Autonomous speaking remains disabled.
Spoken output must respect evidence gaps and human-review gates.
surfaces.nextStep: Select provider only after governed response, consent, and interruption policies are approved.

guardrails: The current report and default scoped chat remain stable unless a user explicitly opts into the governed runtime.
Every governed-ready surface must expose proof, gates, evidence, and source caveats.
Realtime voice, continuous listening, and TTS remain gated until runtime parity, consent, privacy, and interruption behavior are approved.
caveats: This is a prototype-local registry, not an enterprise operations console.
Ready surfaces are ready for governed prototype use, not approved production deployment.
Approved source-owner data is still required before Momentum Intelligence can be treated as canonical executive source truth.
